pattern: The property is monotone in k, so binary-search the answer and for each candidate mark the first k removable indices and verify p is still a subsequence of the surviving characters.
hints: If removing the first k indices keeps p a subsequence, removing any fewer also does, so the feasible k form a prefix.
Binary-search the largest valid k; the check is a single subsequence test over s with the first k removable positions deleted.
For a candidate k, build a boolean removed set of those indices and run a two-pointer subsequence match skipping removed characters.

edge_inputs.description: two removals keep p a subsequence
p equals s, removing non-essential duplicates
removable order matters, later index needed first

stress.description: random s with p a guaranteed subsequence and a random removable permutation subset
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 400)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    # pick p as a random subsequence (non-empty)
    idxs = sorted(rng.sample(range(n), rng.randint(1, n)))
    p = ''.join(s[i] for i in idxs)
    # removable is a subset of indices, shuffled
    rcount = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    rem = rng.sample(range(n), rcount)
    rng.shuffle(rem)
    return [s, p, rem]
stress.note: p always a valid subsequence by construction

stress.description: small alphabet long strings to stress the subsequence checks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(50, 600)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(n))
    idxs = sorted(rng.sample(range(n), rng.randint(1, n)))
    p = ''.join(s[i] for i in idxs)
    rcount = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    rem = rng.sample(range(n), rcount)
    rng.shuffle(rem)
    return [s, p, rem]
